  "text": "SASL mechanism configuration validation at broker startup in apache/kafka is spread across several files:\n\n1. **`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`** — The primary broker configuration class. The private `saslEnabledMechanisms(listenerName)` method reads the `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` config (BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG) for a given listener. The `validateValues()` method (called during KafkaConfig construction) enforces that when SASL is used for inter-broker communication, the `sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol` value must be present in `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` (line 628: `require(!interBrokerUsesSasl || saslEnabledMechanisms(...).contains(saslMechanismInterBrokerProtocol), ...)`). This is the top-level sasl.enabled.mechanisms validation at broker startup.\n\n2. **`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java`** — The `serverChannelBuilder()` method reads `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` from the broker config and, for each enabled mechanism, calls `JaasContext.loadServerContext(listenerName, mechanism, configs)` (lines 137–140). This is where the JAAS config presence check is triggered for each enabled mechanism on each listener.\n\n3. **`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java`** — The `loadServerContext()` static method performs the JAAS config presence check. It looks for a mechanism-prefixed `sasl.jaas.config` property (dynamic config) or falls back to the static JAAS configuration file. If neither is found, it throws an `IllegalArgumentException` with `\"Could not find a '...' entry in the JAAS configuration file\"` (line 147–150). This is the core JAAS config presence validation.\n\n4. **`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java`** — The `configure()` method initializes mechanism-specific components. It handles GSSAPI specially by calling `maybeAddNativeGssapiCredentials()` (line 155). The `createServerCallbackHandlers()` method (lines 318–334) selects the appropriate callback handler per mechanism: `ScramServerCallbackHandler` for SCRAM mechanisms, `OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler` for OAUTHBEARER, and `PlainServerCallbackHandler` for PLAIN. This is where mechanism-specific (SCRAM, GSSAPI, OAUTHBEARER) configuration validation occurs.\n\n5. **`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java`** — The constructor validates that the list of enabled mechanisms is non-empty (throws `IllegalArgumentException(\"No SASL mechanisms are enabled\")` at line 180) and that each mechanism has an associated callback handler and subject (lines 182–186).\n\n6. **`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java`** — Defines the `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` constant and its default value (GSSAPI), used throughout the validation chain.\n\nThe validation chain at broker startup flows: `KafkaConfig.validateValues()` → `ChannelBuilders.serverChannelBuilder()` → `JaasContext.loadServerContext()` → `SaslChannelBuilder.configure()` → `SaslServerAuthenticator` constructor."

## SASL Mechanism Configuration Validation at Broker Startup

The validation is spread across these key files in `apache/kafka`:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` | **`sasl.enabled.mechanisms` validation** — `validateValues()` enforces that the inter-broker SASL mechanism is in the enabled list; `saslEnabledMechanisms()` reads the per-listener config |
| `clients/.../network/ChannelBuilders.java` | **JAAS config trigger** — `serverChannelBuilder()` iterates enabled mechanisms and calls `JaasContext.loadServerContext()` for each |
| `clients/.../security/JaasContext.java` | **JAAS config presence check** — `loadServerContext()` throws `IllegalArgumentException` if no JAAS config entry is found for a mechanism |
| `clients/.../network/SaslChannelBuilder.java` | **Mechanism-specific config** — `configure()` handles GSSAPI credentials; `createServerCallbackHandlers()` assigns SCRAM/GSSAPI/OAUTHBEARER-specific callback handlers |
| `clients/.../authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java` | **Runtime validation** — constructor checks that mechanisms list is non-empty and each mechanism has a callback handler and subject |
| `clients/.../config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java` | Defines `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` constant used throughout |

This is where the JAAS config presence check is triggered for each enabled mechanism on each listener.\n\n3. **`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java`** — The `loadServerContext()` static method performs the JAAS config presence check. It looks for a mechanism-prefixed `sasl.jaas.config` property (dynamic config) or falls back to the static JAAS configuration file.
